original_image_tags: Stamped box in lower right corner. Top line: "FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION". Second line: "U.S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE". Date stamp: "JUL 30 1947". Location: "SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA". Below location: "ROUTED TO". To the right of "ROUTED TO": a stamped initial "R".

elements.description: Typed memorandum detailing a sighting of five or six white circular objects by 1st Lt Eric B. Armstrong on 28 June 1947. It includes a header 'UNIDENTIFIABLE OBJECTS Williams Fld., Chandler, Ariz. 15 July 1947', the body of the interview, 'AGENT'S NOTES', and a signature block for Lawrence R. King, Jr.

A rectangular stamp in the lower right corner that reads 'FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION U.S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE JUL 30 1947 SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA ROUTED TO' with a handwritten initial or symbol 'W' below it, and a handwritten number '62-2938-11' at the very bottom edge.
A certification stamp or typed section in the lower left that reads 'CERTIFIED A TRUE COPY: R. D. STEPHENS Major, Air Corps' with a handwritten signature above the name.

judge.reason: Description B describes a typed memorandum as the primary visual content, while Description A only describes a stamp, and they also contradict on the initial within the stamp ('R' vs 'W').
